title: MISTAKE: I treated filesystem isolation as sufficient and attempted t
content:
What went wrong: I treated filesystem isolation as sufficient and attempted to push to a branch that another agent could also be updating. That risks racing collaborators even when using a separate worktree.
How to avoid: Always create a unique branch per implementation worktree. Never push to a shared active branch. If the remote branch moves, stop and re-integrate onto a fresh isolated branch before pushing.
Rubric weighted score: 0

title: MISTAKE: I implied end-user delivery certainty from CI success. Workf
content:
What went wrong: I implied end-user delivery certainty from CI success. Workflow success is evidence of distribution command success, not proof of email notification or receipt.
How to avoid: When reporting release/distribution outcomes, distinguish build/upload success from actual notification receipt. Require direct verification of release presence or tester assignment before claiming delivery.
Rubric weighted score: 0
Guardrails failed: testsPassed

title: MISTAKE: User gave thumbs down after I overstated delivery certainty
content:
Context: User gave thumbs down after I overstated delivery certainty from CI success for Firebase/TestFlight without verifying actual release presence or email receipt.
Action needed: investigate and prevent recurrence
